description_long: This flower features a striking, bulbous bloom with a pointed tip and a dramatic pattern of red and white stripes radiating from the center. The outer surface is predominantly deep red with bold, creamy white markings, giving it a visually arresting appearance. The lower part of the flower curves downward, forming a tubular structure that appears to open slightly, revealing a lighter, almost translucent interior. The surface texture looks velvety and soft, with subtle undulations and a matte finish. No leaves are visible, and the flower appears to be solitary, with a slender, tapering base that may connect to a stem. The overall form is symmetrical along one axis, with petals fused or closely overlapping in a bilateral arrangement.
description: A striking, red and white striped flower with a bulbous, pointed bloom and a curved, tubular lower section. The surface is velvety and matte, with a bold, symmetrical pattern.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full bulbous bloom with prominent red and white stripes and the open, curved lower section.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated, tapering base and the flower's overall slender profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the pointed tip and the symmetrical, radiating stripe pattern on the back of the bloom.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the curvature of the lower section and the continuity of the striped pattern.
attribute_petal_arrangement: Bilateral arrangement with petals fused or closely overlapping, forming a symmetrical, tubular structure with a pointed tip and radiating stripe pattern.
